title: Quantization
text: Quantization reduces the inference cost of large language models by storing weights in lower precision such as int8 or int4. It cuts memory and latency with little accuracy loss.

title: Knowledge distillation
text: Knowledge distillation reduces inference cost by training a smaller student model to imitate a larger teacher. You then deploy the cheaper student at similar quality.

title: Mixture of experts
text: Mixture of experts lowers the inference cost of large language models by activating only a few expert subnetworks per token instead of the whole network.

title: KV caching
text: Caching key and value attention states between decoding steps reduces the inference cost of language models by avoiding repeated computation.

title: Chunking
text: Chunking splits documents into passages so retrieval augmented generation can fetch focused context. Chunk size strongly affects how reliable the answers are.

title: Reranking
text: Reranking reorders retrieved passages with a cross encoder so the most relevant context reaches the model, making retrieval augmented generation more reliable.

title: Hybrid retrieval
text: Hybrid retrieval combines keyword BM25 and dense vector search. It improves recall and makes retrieval augmented generation more reliable across query types.

title: Grounding and citations
text: Grounding answers in retrieved sources and attaching citations makes retrieval augmented generation more reliable and lets users verify claims.

title: Benchmarks
text: Standard benchmarks evaluate a large language model on fixed tasks, giving comparable scores but risking data contamination over time.

title: LLM as a judge
text: LLM as a judge uses a strong model to evaluate the outputs of another language model, scaling evaluation beyond what human raters can cover.

title: Human evaluation
text: Human evaluation remains a gold standard to evaluate a language model on helpfulness and safety, though it is slow and costly.

title: Red teaming
text: Red teaming evaluates a language model by adversarially probing for unsafe or policy violating responses before deployment.

title: LoRA
text: LoRA fine-tunes language models efficiently by training small low rank adapter matrices while freezing the base weights.

title: QLoRA
text: QLoRA fine-tunes language models efficiently by combining four bit quantization with low rank adapters, cutting memory enough to tune on a single GPU.

title: Adapter tuning
text: Parameter efficient fine tuning inserts small adapter layers so you fine-tune language models efficiently without updating all parameters.

title: Prompt injection defense
text: Defending against prompt injection in an LLM application uses input validation, guardrails, least privilege for tools, and output filtering.

title: Guardrails
text: Guardrails screen the inputs and outputs of an LLM application, blocking prompt injection and unsafe content before it reaches tools or users.

title: Least privilege for tools
text: Granting tools least privilege limits the damage a successful prompt injection can do inside an LLM application.
